statusLine: EU fuel reserves under strain with 17 of 27 countries holding critical-level stocks in at least one fuel type, while oil prices climb near $97 per barrel.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as stock levels remain below optimal across multiple countries and fuel types. While EU-wide averages for petrol (94.5 days) and diesel (95.8 days) hover just above the 90-day mandatory minimum, jet fuel reserves at 89.9 days fall short of requirements. This aggregate picture masks severe disparities, with 17 of 27 EU countries experiencing critical shortages in at least one fuel category.

The most vulnerable nations include Romania, where all three fuel types sit at critical levels with diesel stocks dangerously low at just 20.2 days. Poland and Spain similarly face critical shortages across all categories, while major economies like Germany and France show mixed results—Germany maintains adequate petrol stocks (107 days) but critical diesel reserves (63.9 days). Eastern European countries appear particularly strained, likely reflecting ongoing supply chain pressures from regional geopolitical tensions.

Oil market conditions add further pressure, with Brent crude climbing to $96.81 per barrel—up 0.93% and approximately 27% higher since the current Middle East conflict began. This price surge directly impacts fuel costs, with significant price variations across the EU ranging from Malta's €1.34 per liter for petrol to the Netherlands' €2.33. The combination of tight physical stocks and elevated crude prices creates a challenging environment for European fuel security, requiring careful monitoring and potential policy intervention to prevent supply disruptions.
keyPoints: 17 of 27 EU countries face critical fuel shortages in at least one category, with Romania, Poland, and Spain most severely affected across all fuel types
EU jet fuel reserves at 89.9 days fall below the 90-day mandatory minimum, while petrol and diesel averages barely exceed requirements
Brent crude oil has surged 27% since the Middle East conflict began, reaching $96.81 per barrel and driving fuel price increases across Europe
Eastern European nations show particular vulnerability, with countries like Romania holding just 20.2 days of diesel reserves
Fuel price disparities span from €1.34 per liter in Malta to €2.33 in the Netherlands, reflecting varying tax policies and supply chain costs
